title: Instruction Tuning
concept: Instruction tuning trains models on collections of formatted prompt-response tasks, teaching them to follow natural language commands directly.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary thing a raw language model learns to do during its initial training phase?

options.text: Predict the next word in a sequence

exercises.explain: Traditional AI training teaches models to predict the next word in a sentence, which makes them great at autocompleting text but not necessarily at acting like helpful assistants.

cards.title: Why Won't AI Listen?
cards.body: Have you ever tried to ask a basic AI a question, and it just repeated your question back to you? That happens because raw AI models only know how to predict the next word. To make them helpful assistants, we need a special training step!

cards.title: What is Instruction Tuning?
cards.body: Instruction tuning is the process of training a base AI model using structured examples of prompts (instructions) and their correct answers. This teaches the model how to read a command and perform the exact task requested.

cards.title: The Puppy Training School
cards.body: Imagine training a puppy. Instead of just letting it watch you live, you give it clear, direct commands like "Sit" or "Roll over" and reward it when it follows them. Instruction tuning is like teaching a model how to listen to commands instead of just guessing the next word.

cards.title: Learning by Example
cards.body:
Instead of just feeding a model random internet text, we show it pairs like:

• **Prompt:** "Translate 'Hello' to French."
• **Response:** "Bonjour."

By training on thousands of these pairs, the model learns the pattern of following instructions.

cards.title: Myth: AI is Born Helpful
cards.body: People often think AI models naturally know how to be helpful assistants out of the box. In reality, a raw AI model just tries to complete text. It requires instruction tuning to learn how to actually follow commands and answer questions.

cards.title: Autocomplete vs. Assistant
cards.body: See how the behavior changes before and after an AI model goes through instruction tuning.

right.text: Input: 'Write a poem about dogs.' -> Output: 'Loyal and sweet, with four quick paws, they run to greet...'

left.text: Input: 'Write a poem about dogs.' -> Output: 'Write a poem about cats. Write a poem about birds.'

exercises.prompt: If you give a raw base model (without instruction tuning) the prompt: "Translate 'Hello' to Spanish.", what is it most likely to do?

options.text: It writes a story about translating text.
It might just autocomplete the prompt with: "Translate 'Good morning' to Spanish."
It automatically opens a translation app.
It shuts down because it gets confused.

exercises.explain: A raw language model (base model) only knows how to autocomplete text. Instruction tuning is what teaches it to recognize and answer a prompt.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Instruction tuning requires developers to rewrite the entire AI program from scratch.
exercises.explain: Instruction tuning does not require rewriting the core code; it is a training process where the model learns from a dataset of instruction-and-response examples.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how instruction tuning helps language models interact with humans.
exercises.sentence: Instruction tuning trains models on prompt-response pairs so they learn to follow natural language [blank].
exercises.explain: Instruction tuning uses structured prompt-response pairs to teach models how to listen to and follow human commands.

exercises.prompt: Identify which response shows an AI that has successfully undergone instruction tuning:

options.text: AI autocomplete: "...and write a summary of it for your homework."
AI Assistant: "Here is a brief summary of the book: it explores themes of adventure..."
AI autocomplete: "Summarize this book. Summarize this article. Summarize this movie."

exercises.explain: The AI Assistant is showing the correct behavior of instruction tuning by directly following the command to summarize, rather than just autocompleting the sentence.

exercises.prompt: Why is instruction tuning considered a game-changer for making AI models useful in daily life?

options.text: It makes the model run much faster on mobile phones.
It helps the model memorize every single website on the internet.
It teaches the model to behave like an assistant that follows directions.
It reduces the amount of computer power needed to train the model from scratch.

exercises.explain: Without instruction tuning, a language model acts like a text autocomplete engine—it just tries to continue your sentence. Instruction tuning is the crucial step that transforms it into a helpful assistant that understands and carries out tasks.
